---
title: "About"
image: assets/avatar.jpg
image-alt: "Portrait of Matej Gazda"
about:
  template: solana
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/BraveDistribution
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/matej-gazda-579a36100/
toc: false
---

I am Matej Gazda, an **AI researcher and consultant** working on machine
learning for medical imaging: how to build models that are accurate,
calibrated, and trustworthy in clinical settings where data is scarce and the
cost of being wrong is high.

## Research interests

- Deep learning for medical image analysis
- Sample-efficient learning, self-supervision, and domain adaptation
- Uncertainty quantification and model calibration
- The boundary between methodological novelty and clinical usefulness

## Background

I moved into AI research after several years in software engineering, where I
worked on production ML systems and distributed services. That mix shows up
here: most of what I write tries to be both rigorous and runnable.

## This site

The blog archive (`tag: legacy`) contains the earliest posts from 2017–2019,
ported from the WordPress version of this site. Everything new will live in
[Writing](posts/index.qmd).

## Get in touch

- GitHub: [BraveDistribution](https://github.com/BraveDistribution)
- LinkedIn: [matej-gazda](https://www.linkedin.com/in/matej-gazda-579a36100/)
